tool.name: add_attack_node
tool.description:
Add or update a node in the attack graph — a strategic map of your scan progress.
Use this to plan attack paths, track progress on each step, and build a clear picture of the engagement. Each node represents an action (recon, exploit, pivot, etc.) and its status.

parameter.name: node_type
parameter.description: Type: recon, enumerate, vulnerability, exploit, credential, access, pivot, escalation, exfiltration, persistence

parameter.name: description
parameter.description: What this step does or found (e.g. "Port scan 10.0.0.1 — found SSH, HTTP, MySQL")

parameter.name: status
parameter.description: Status: planned, in_progress, success, failed, blocked, skipped. Default: planned

parameter.name: parent_id
parameter.description: ID of the parent node (creates an edge in the graph)

parameter.name: technique
parameter.description: MITRE ATT&CK technique or tool name

parameter.name: evidence
parameter.description: Key evidence or output from this step (max 500 chars)

parameter.name: priority
parameter.description: Priority 1-10 (10 = highest). Used by suggest_next_action. Default: 5

tool.examples:
# Plan initial recon
recon-nmap recon Nmap scan of 10.0.0.1 — discover open ports and services planned 10.0.0.1 8
# Record a found vulnerability
vuln-sqli-login vulnerability SQL injection in /login endpoint, parameter 'username' success recon-nmap https://10.0.0.1/login T1190 sqlmap confirmed: MySQL 5.7, injectable via username param
# Plan exploitation based on finding
exploit-sqli-dump exploit Dump user credentials via SQL injection planned vuln-sqli-login 9

tool.name: get_attack_graph
tool.description:
Get the current state of the attack graph — all nodes, edges, and statistics.
Returns a tree visualization, stats by type and status, and full node data.

returns.description: Graph state with tree visualization, stats, nodes, and edges

tool.name: suggest_next_action
tool.description:
Analyze the attack graph and suggest the highest-priority next action.
Looks for: unexploited vulnerabilities, unused credentials, missing escalation attempts, and planned actions. Use this when deciding what to do next.

returns.description: Prioritized suggestions with reasoning and related node IDs
